column2: Users can reach a server by IP address, but the friendly hostname fails. Which network service should be investigated first?
column5:
Why

DNS translates names and IP addresses. A name-only failure points toward the name-resolution service path.

Related Cards

Objective 2.1 covers common DNS port recall. This card tests network-service symptom recognition.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario DNS NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: A workstation should receive network settings automatically, but it never receives an address from the central service. Which service should be checked?
column5:
Why

DHCP provides automatic IP address configuration. In larger environments it is commonly centralized and redundant.

Exam Trap

Do not turn this into manual static addressing until the automatic configuration service has been considered.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario DHCP NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: Authentication events and log timestamps do not line up across systems. Which network service should be checked for synchronized clocks?
column5:
Why

Time synchronization supports authentication, encryption, backups, log timestamps, and troubleshooting timelines.

Real Help Desk

When logs disagree, verify time before assuming the events happened in the order shown.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario NTP TimeSync Source::Messer-v170

column2: A department needs centralized storage where users can copy, rename, and delete shared documents. Which network service fits this need?
column5:
Why

File shares centralize access to files while the user interface hides the underlying protocol details.

Exam Trap

Do not confuse a file share with a database just because both store information.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario FileShare NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: Multiple network devices need to send jobs to one shared printer. Which network service provides shared printing?
column5:
Why

A print server provides printing services for network devices. It may be software on a computer or built into the printer.

Real Help Desk

When every user loses access to the same printer, check the shared print service path before troubleshooting each workstation separately.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario PrintServer NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: Users need a service that stores incoming email and handles outgoing email. Which network service is involved?
column5:
Why

Mail servers store incoming mail and send outgoing mail. They are often managed by an ISP or IT department.

Related Cards

Objective 2.1 covers mail protocol port associations; this card tests service purpose.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario MailServer NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: A browser requests a page, and a server responds with web content. Which network service is providing the page?
column5:
Why

Web servers respond to browser requests and provide pages to clients.

Exam Trap

Do not turn this into HTTP/HTTPS port recall; Objective 2.3 is testing the service role.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario WebServer NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: An enterprise wants centralized login authentication for access to multiple resources. Which type of network service is needed?
column5:
Why

Authentication servers centralize login authentication to resources and are commonly enterprise services.

Real Help Desk

When many unrelated resources reject valid users, check the central authentication path.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario Authentication NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: An application needs a service that stores structured information in tables and lets related data be linked together. Which service type is this?
column5:
Why

Database servers store structured data. Relational databases organize data in tables and link related information.

Source Note

This card avoids vendor-specific database product memorization.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario Database NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: A security team wants diverse devices to send event messages to one central logging receiver. Which network service fits this requirement?
column5:
Why

Syslog centralizes message logging from diverse systems. Central logs help troubleshooting and security review.

Exam Trap

Do not confuse centralized logging with centralized authentication. They solve different problems.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.9; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario Syslog Logging Source::Messer-v170

column2: Clients should send web requests to an intermediate server that fetches the content and returns it, while also allowing filtering and caching. What service is this?
column5:
Why

A proxy performs requests on behalf of clients and may provide access control, caching, URL filtering, or content scanning.

Exam Trap

The proxy is not the final destination service; it sits between the client and destination.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario Proxy NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: A website uses several back-end servers, but users connect to one front-end service that distributes requests. What network service is providing this distribution?
column5:
Why

Load balancers distribute requests across multiple servers and can help keep services available when one server fails.

Exam Trap

Do not confuse a load balancer with a backup. It distributes active service traffic.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario LoadBalancer Availability Source::Messer-v170

column2: An organization wants to filter unsolicited and phishing email before it reaches users' mailboxes. Which service should handle that filtering?
column5:
Why

Spam gateways filter unwanted or malicious email before it reaches the user.

Source Note

This card tests the service purpose, not whether the gateway is on-site or cloud-based.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario SpamGateway Email Source::Messer-v170

column2: A small organization wants one appliance that combines firewalling, filtering, malware inspection, VPN endpoint, and other security functions. What class of device fits this description?
column4: An all-in-one security appliance, such as a UTM-style device.
column5:
Why

All-in-one security appliances consolidate multiple security and network functions into one platform.

Exam Trap

Do not memorize an exhaustive feature list. Recognize the consolidated security-appliance role.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario UTM SecurityAppliance Source::Messer-v170

column2: A network supports industrial control equipment for manufacturing and must not be reachable from the outside. Which service environment is this most likely describing?
column5:
Why

SCADA and ICS environments manage industrial equipment and require strong segmentation.

Exam Trap

Treat these systems as safety- and operations-critical. Broad outside access is a serious design concern.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario SCADA ICS Source::Messer-v170

column2: A very old system still runs an important business process. What term describes this kind of older but still important system?
column5:
Why

Legacy systems are older systems that may still be important. Supporting old technology can still be operationally necessary.

Exam Trap

Old does not automatically mean unimportant or safe to remove.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario LegacySystems NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: A door-security controller is purpose-built and does not normally provide direct operating-system access. What type of system is this?
column5:
Why

Embedded systems are purpose-built devices. Direct access to a general-purpose operating system is not typical.

Related Cards

2.3-B016 covers older legacy systems. This card tests purpose-built embedded systems.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic Scenario EmbeddedSystems NetworkServices Source::Messer-v170

column2: Smart speakers, thermostats, and doorbells are being added to a network. What design choice can limit damage if one of these devices is compromised?
column5:
Why

IoT devices may require a segmented network to limit the impact of a security breach.

Real Help Desk

Low-cost smart devices should not automatically receive the same network trust as business-critical systems.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.10; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.3
column10: A+::220-1201::2.3 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkServices Basic HighYield Scenario IoT Segmentation Source::Messer-v170
